title: Docker Installation
content:
Title: Docker Installation Guide

Overview:
Docker is a containerization platform that allows you to package applications into containers for easy deployment. This guide explains installation on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Prerequisites:

  • Windows 10/11 64-bit or macOS 10.15+ or Ubuntu 20.04+

  • Internet connection

  • Administrative privileges

Installation Steps:

Windows (Docker Desktop):

  1. Download Docker Desktop from Docker official website.

  2. Run the installer and follow instructions.

  3. Enable WSL 2 backend if prompted.

  4. Verify installation:

macOS (Docker Desktop):

  1. Download the .dmg file from Docker’s website.

  2. Drag Docker to Applications folder.

  3. Launch Docker and complete setup.

  4. Verify installation:

Linux (Ubuntu/Debian):

  1. Update package index:

  2. Install prerequisites:

  3. Add Docker’s official GPG key and repository:

    /dev/null ]]>

  5. Verify installation:

  • Docker Docs

title: Postman Installation
content:
Installing Postman

Title: Postman Installation Guide

Overview:
Postman is a popular API client that helps developers design, test, and monitor APIs. This guide covers installation for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Prerequisites:

  • Internet connection

Installation Steps:

Windows:

  1. Download the Postman installer from Postman’s official website.

  2. Run the .exe file and follow the on-screen instructions.

  3. Launch Postman from Start Menu.

macOS:

  1. Download the Postman .dmg file from official website.

  2. Drag Postman to the Applications folder.

  3. Launch Postman from Applications.

Linux (Ubuntu):

  1. Download the Postman tarball from the official website.

  2. Extract the tarball and move it to /opt:

  3. Launch Postman by running:

References:

  • Postman Documentation

title: Project Documentation: Task Management Web Application
content:
1. Project Overview

1.1 Project Name

TaskMaster Pro – A web application to help users manage tasks, projects, and teams efficiently.

1.2 Purpose

The purpose of this project is to provide a user-friendly platform for individuals and teams to track tasks, manage projects, and improve productivity.

1.3 Scope

  • Task creation, assignment, and tracking

  • Project management with deadlines and milestones

  • Team collaboration features (comments, notifications)

  • Reporting and analytics

  • Integration with calendars and third-party apps (optional)

1.4 Objectives

  • Provide an intuitive UI for task management

  • Enable seamless team collaboration

  • Ensure real-time updates and notifications

  • Maintain data security and privacy

2. Functional Requirements

  1. User Authentication

    • Sign-up, login, logout

    • Password recovery and email verification

  2. Task Management

    • Create, edit, delete tasks

    • Assign tasks to team members

    • Set priority and due dates

  3. Project Management

    • Create projects and milestones

    • Link tasks to projects

    • Track project progress

  4. Notifications

    • Email and in-app notifications for task updates

  5. Reports & Analytics

    • Dashboard showing pending, completed, and overdue tasks

    • Project progress chart

  6. Collaboration

    • Comments on tasks

    • File attachments

3. Non-Functional Requirements

  • Performance: App should handle 500 concurrent users with minimal latency.

  • Scalability: Should support future integration with mobile apps.

  • Security: Encrypt sensitive data (AES encryption), secure API endpoints with OAuth2.

  • Availability: 99.9% uptime.

  • Usability: Intuitive UI with responsive design for mobile and desktop.

4. System Architecture

  • Frontend: React.js with Redux for state management

  • Backend: Node.js with Express.js

  • Database: PostgreSQL

  • Authentication: JWT-based authentication

  • Hosting: AWS EC2 and S3

  • CI/CD: GitHub Actions for automated testing and deployment

Diagram (simplified):

wide760 Backend API (Node.js/Express) <--> PostgreSQL Database | Notifications (SMTP/Push API) ]]>

5. Database Design

5.1 Tables

  1. Users: id, name, email, password_hash, role, created_at

  2. Projects: id, name, description, owner_id, start_date, end_date

  3. Tasks: id, title, description, project_id, assigned_to, status, priority, due_date

  4. Comments: id, task_id, user_id, comment, created_at

5.2 Relationships

  • One user can have many tasks

  • One project can have many tasks

  • Tasks can have multiple comments

6. API Documentation (Sample)

Endpoint

Method

Description

Request Body

Response

/api/users/login

POST

Login user

{email, password}

{token, user}

/api/tasks

GET

Get all tasks

N/A

[{task}]

/api/tasks

POST

Create task

{title, project_id, assigned_to}

{task}

/api/projects

GET

List projects

N/A

[{project}]

7. UI/UX Design

  • Wireframes: Home Dashboard, Project Overview, Task List, Task Detail

  • Design Principles: Minimalistic, responsive, accessible (WCAG 2.1 compliant)

  • Color Scheme: Blue (#1E90FF) for actions, grey (#F0F0F0) for backgrounds

8. Testing Strategy

8.1 Unit Testing

  • Backend: Jest for Node.js

  • Frontend: React Testing Library

8.2 Integration Testing

  • API endpoints using Postman / Newman

8.3 End-to-End Testing

  • Playwright / Selenium for UI workflows

8.4 Performance Testing

  • JMeter for load testing

9. Deployment Plan

  • Development: Local dev environment + Docker containers

  • Staging: AWS EC2 + CI/CD pipeline

  • Production: Load-balanced AWS EC2 instances, RDS database

10. Maintenance and Support

  • Monitor errors using Sentry

  • Regular backups (daily incremental, weekly full)

  • Version control using Git

  • Support tickets via email and chat

11. Risks and Mitigation

Risk

Mitigation

Data loss

Daily backup and replication

Security breach

Penetration testing, encryption

Low adoption

User training and feedback surveys

System downtime

High-availability architecture, monitoring

12. Future Enhancements

  • Mobile application (iOS & Android)

  • AI-based task prioritization

  • Calendar integration with Google and Outlook

  • Advanced analytics and reporting
